original: NASA launches its DART Mission, to test technology to prevent future impact on earth by hazardous asteroid, by deliberately crashing a spacecraft into an asteroid (in a real life echo of the movie Armageddon)
Chinese steward Poon Lim begins 133 days adrift after British ship SS Benlomond is torpedoed by a German U-boat, leaving him as the sole survivor
Apneist Jacques Mayol is the first man to reach a depth of 100m undersea without breathing equipment.
JR Tokai unveil a maglev LO train prototype capable of travelling up to 500km per hour
World of Warcraft, multiplayer online role-playing video game released, world's most subscribed game
European Space Agency is the first to announce the inclusion of a disabled person amongst their new class of astronauts - Britain's John McFall
Manuscript of early workings ofAlbert Einstein's theory of relativity "one of the most important scientific documents of the 20th century" sells at auction for $13 million
Charli D'Ameliobecomes the first person to hit 100 million followers on TikTok
Sumatran rhino officially declared extinct in Malaysia after last known specimen, 25-year-old Iman, dies of cancer in Sabah, Malaysian Borneo
Debut of "Doctor Who" the long-running British sci-fi series
China launches Chang’e-5 mission to the Moon from Wenchang space site, Hainan Island, to collect lunar rock and soil samples
Clipper Cutty Sark is launched in Dumbarton, Scotland, as one of the last clippers ever built and the only one still surviving
Lisa Lindahl, American inventor of the sports bra (Jogbra), born in Montclair, New Jersey[1]
Pierre de Coubertin launches plan for modern Olympic Games at the Union des Sociétés Françaises de Sports Athlétiques AGM
Debut of 1st jukebox (Palais Royale Saloon, San Francisco)
Egypt reopens its 3000 year old Avenue of the Sphinxes in Luxor with a grand ceremony
Boston College QBDoug Flutiepasses (472 yards), including game ending 48 yard TD (Hail Mary Pass) to end game & beat Miami 47-45
Wayne Gretzkyscores his 600th NHL goal
Just past midnight, a sharp jolt likely caused by heavy rains causes Lake Merced, California, to drop 30 feet (9m)
Maurice Flitcroft, British golfer known as "the world's worst golfer", born in Manchester, England (d. 2007)
"The Day of the Doctor" 50th anniversary episode of "Doctor Who" screens on BBC One, 1st episode to feature 12th Doctor Peter Capaldi
TV series "Wednesday" produced byTim Burton, starringJenna Ortegaand Gwendoline Christie released on Netflix
Harpo Marx [Adolph], American actor and comedian (Marx brothers), born in New York City (d. 1964)
Portable pencil sharpener patented by American inventor John Lee Love
European weather satellite Meteosat 1 launched from Cape Canaveral
MS Explorer, a cruise liner sinks in the Antarctic Ocean south of Argentina after hitting an ice field near the South Shetland Islands; all 154 passengers and crew were rescued

St Clemens Flood: Storm surge overwhelms dikes along Flemish and Dutch North Sea coast, killing thousands; city of Ostend moves church inland to higher ground (an effort repeated twice more over the next two centuries)
AstraZeneca is the third drugmaker to report an effective vaccine for COVID-19 (62% or 90% effective depending on how it is given) and easy-to make and distribute

Phil Mickelsonclaims $9m winner-takes-all prize in dramatic matchplay golf duel withTiger Woods, finishing under floodlights in Las Vegas; match goes to 22nd hole before Mickelson sinks 4-foot putt for rich victory
British Mercedes driverLewis Hamiltonwins season-ending Abu Dhabi Grand Prix at the Yas Marina Circuit to clinch his second F1 World Drivers Championship, 67 points ahead of teammate Nico Rosberg

Dallas Cowboys defensive back DaRon Bland breaks NFL single-season record for interception returns for a touchdown with his fifth, in 45-10 win over visiting Washington Commanders; previous record of 4 set in 1971 and most recently tied in 1993
Filipino boxerManny Pacquiaodefeats American Brandon Rios by unanimous decision to win the WBO welterweight title at Cotai Arena, Macau, China
Robin Roberts, American television reporter (Good Morning America), born in Tuskegee, Alabama
Ludovico Einaudi, Italian pianist, classical and film composer (Divenire), born in Turin, Piedmont, Italy
Norman Hunter, English children's writer (Professor Branestawm), born in London (d. 1995)
Wright Brothers form million dollar corporation to manufacture airplanes
1st issue of Life picture magazine created by Henry R. Luce published
4,800 die in series of earthquakes that devastated southern Italy
A magnitude 6.7 earthquake strikes central Japan
Tiros 2, a weather satellite is launched
Vladislav Volkov, Soviet-Russian cosmonaut (Soyuz 7, 11), born in Moscow, Russia (d. 1971)
David Rappaport, English 3'11" actor (Wizard, Time Bandits), born in London, England
Legendary Delta blues musicianRobert Johnson's first recording session with producer Don Law at the Gunter Hotel, San Antonio, Texas
The Beatles film promotional video clips for 5 of their songs at Twickenham Film Studios to provide to television outlets (in lieu of live appearances)
Susan Boylereleases her debut album “I Dreamed a Dream” (biggest selling album worldwide 2009, 2010 Billboard Album of the Year)
George Harrisonreleases "My Sweet Lord" single in the US
"A Christmas Carol" directed by Clive Donner starringGeorge C. Scottpremieres in the UK
Dr Frank G. Back (NYC) patents lens to provide zoom effects
Louis Ducos du Hauron patents a process of making color photographs, in Paris, France
7.17" (18.2 cm) of rainfall, Rattlesnake Creek, Idaho (state record)
Martín López-Zubero swims world record 200m backstroke (1:56.57)
Brigham Young University quarterback Ty Detmer finishes NCAA career with record 4,031 yards passed in a season, and 15,031 yards for career
Limited-over international cricket debut forWasim Akramfor Pakistan, v New Zealand
Evander HolyfieldTKOs Bert Cooper in 7 for heavyweight boxing title
Second WTA Tour Championship of the year; change of tennis schedule;Martina Navratilovawins 5th straight and 8th overall title with 7–6, 6–3, 6–2 win overSteffi Grafin NYC
Czech tennis No. 1 Jana Novotná wins WTA Tour Championship at Madison Square Garden, NYC; beatsMary Pierceof France 7–6, 6–2, 6–3
Sacramento Kings ends NBA's longest road losing streak at 43 games
Alexis Argüello of Nicaragua knocks out Mexican defending champion Rubén Olivares in the 13th round at the Forum in Inglewood, California, to claim the WBA world featherweight boxing title
Beatles release single "I Feel Fine" backed with "She's a Woman"
Soyuz T-9 lands
